speaker: DAUPHIN
text: My Lord Melun, let this be copied out,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And keep it safe for our remembrance.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Return the precedent to these lords again,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That having our fair order written down,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Both they and we, perusing o’er these notes,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: May know wherefore we took the Sacrament,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And keep our faiths firm and inviolable.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Upon our sides it never shall be broken.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And, noble dauphin, albeit we swear

speaker: SALISBURY
text: A voluntary zeal and unurged faith

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To your proceedings, yet believe me, prince,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: I am not glad that such a sore of time

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Should seek a plaster by contemned revolt

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And heal the inveterate canker of one wound

speaker: SALISBURY
text: By making many. O, it grieves my soul

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That I must draw this metal from my side

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To be a widow-maker! O, and there

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Where honorable rescue and defense

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Cries out upon the name of Salisbury!

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But such is the infection of the time

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That for the health and physic of our right,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: We cannot deal but with the very hand

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Of stern injustice and confusèd wrong.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And is ’t not pity, O my grievèd friends,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That we, the sons and children of this isle,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Was born to see so sad an hour as this,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Wherein we step after a stranger, march

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Upon her gentle bosom, and fill up

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Her enemies’ ranks? I must withdraw and weep

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Upon the spot of this enforcèd cause,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To grace the gentry of a land remote,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And follow unacquainted colors here.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: What, here? O nation, that thou couldst remove,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That Neptune’s arms, who clippeth thee about,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Would bear thee from the knowledge of thyself

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And grapple thee unto a pagan shore,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Where these two Christian armies might combine

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The blood of malice in a vein of league,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And not to spend it so unneighborly.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: A noble temper dost thou show in this,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And great affections wrestling in thy bosom

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Doth make an earthquake of nobility.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: O, what a noble combat hast thou fought

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Between compulsion and a brave respect!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Let me wipe off this honorable dew

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That silverly doth progress on thy cheeks.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: My heart hath melted at a lady’s tears,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Being an ordinary inundation,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: But this effusion of such manly drops,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: This shower, blown up by tempest of the soul,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Startles mine eyes and makes me more amazed

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Than had I seen the vaulty top of heaven

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Figured quite o’er with burning meteors.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Lift up thy brow, renownèd Salisbury,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And with a great heart heave away this storm.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Commend these waters to those baby eyes

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That never saw the giant world enraged,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Nor met with fortune other than at feasts

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Full warm of blood, of mirth, of gossiping.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Come, come; for thou shalt thrust thy hand as deep

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Into the purse of rich prosperity

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As Louis himself.—So, nobles, shall you all,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That knit your sinews to the strength of mine.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And even there, methinks, an angel spake.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Look where the holy legate comes apace

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To give us warrant from the hand of God,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And on our actions set the name of right

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: With holy breath.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Hail, noble prince of France.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The next is this: King John hath reconciled

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Himself to Rome; his spirit is come in

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That so stood out against the holy Church,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The great metropolis and See of Rome.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Therefore thy threat’ning colors now wind up,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And tame the savage spirit of wild war

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That, like a lion fostered up at hand,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: It may lie gently at the foot of peace

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And be no further harmful than in show.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Your Grace shall pardon me; I will not back.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I am too high-born to be propertied,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To be a secondary at control,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Or useful servingman and instrument

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To any sovereign state throughout the world.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Your breath first kindled the dead coal of wars

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Between this chastised kingdom and myself

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And brought in matter that should feed this fire;

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And now ’tis far too huge to be blown out

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: With that same weak wind which enkindled it.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: You taught me how to know the face of right,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Acquainted me with interest to this land,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Yea, thrust this enterprise into my heart.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And come you now to tell me John hath made

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: His peace with Rome? What is that peace to me?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I, by the honor of my marriage bed,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: After young Arthur claim this land for mine.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And now it is half conquered, must I back

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Because that John hath made his peace with Rome?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Am I Rome’s slave? What penny hath Rome borne?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: What men provided? What munition sent

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To underprop this action? Is ’t not I

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That undergo this charge? Who else but I,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And such as to my claim are liable,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Sweat in this business and maintain this war?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Have I not heard these islanders shout out

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: “Vive le Roi” as I have banked their towns?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Have I not here the best cards for the game

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To win this easy match played for a crown?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And shall I now give o’er the yielded set?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: No, no, on my soul, it never shall be said.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: You look but on the outside of this work.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Outside or inside, I will not return

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Till my attempt so much be glorified

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As to my ample hope was promisèd

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Before I drew this gallant head of war

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And culled these fiery spirits from the world

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To outlook conquest and to win renown

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Even in the jaws of danger and of death.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: What lusty trumpet thus doth summon us?

speaker: BASTARD
text: According to the fair play of the world,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Let me have audience. I am sent to speak,

speaker: BASTARD
text: My holy lord of Milan, from the King.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I come to learn how you have dealt for him,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And, as you answer, I do know the scope

speaker: BASTARD
text: And warrant limited unto my tongue.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The Dauphin is too willful-opposite

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And will not temporize with my entreaties.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: He flatly says he’ll not lay down his arms.

speaker: BASTARD
text: By all the blood that ever fury breathed,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The youth says well! Now hear our English king,

speaker: BASTARD
text: For thus his royalty doth speak in me:

speaker: BASTARD
text: He is prepared—and reason too he should.

speaker: BASTARD
text: This apish and unmannerly approach,

speaker: BASTARD
text: This harnessed masque and unadvisèd revel,

speaker: BASTARD
text: This unheard sauciness and boyish troops,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The King doth smile at, and is well prepared

speaker: BASTARD
text: To whip this dwarfish war, these pigmy arms,

speaker: BASTARD
text: From out the circle of his territories.

speaker: BASTARD
text: That hand which had the strength, even at your door,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To cudgel you and make you take the hatch,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To dive like buckets in concealèd wells,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To crouch in litter of your stable planks,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To lie like pawns locked up in chests and trunks,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To hug with swine, to seek sweet safety out

speaker: BASTARD
text: In vaults and prisons, and to thrill and shake

speaker: BASTARD
text: Even at the crying of your nation’s crow,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Thinking this voice an armèd Englishman—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Shall that victorious hand be feebled here

speaker: BASTARD
text: That in your chambers gave you chastisement?

speaker: BASTARD
text: No! Know the gallant monarch is in arms,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And like an eagle o’er his aerie towers

speaker: BASTARD
text: To souse annoyance that comes near his nest.—

speaker: BASTARD
text: And you degenerate, you ingrate revolts,

speaker: BASTARD
text: You bloody Neroes, ripping up the womb

speaker: BASTARD
text: Of your dear mother England, blush for shame!

speaker: BASTARD
text: For your own ladies and pale-visaged maids

speaker: BASTARD
text: Like Amazons come tripping after drums,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Their thimbles into armèd gauntlets change,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Their needles to lances, and their gentle hearts

speaker: BASTARD
text: To fierce and bloody inclination.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: There end thy brave and turn thy face in peace.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: We grant thou canst outscold us. Fare thee well.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: We hold our time too precious to be spent

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: With such a brabbler.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Give me leave to speak.

speaker: BASTARD
text: No, I will speak.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: We will attend to neither.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Strike up the drums, and let the tongue of war

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Plead for our interest and our being here.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Indeed, your drums being beaten will cry out,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And so shall you, being beaten. Do but start

speaker: BASTARD
text: An echo with the clamor of thy drum,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And even at hand a drum is ready braced

speaker: BASTARD
text: That shall reverberate all as loud as thine.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sound but another, and another shall,

speaker: BASTARD
text: As loud as thine, rattle the welkin’s ear

speaker: BASTARD
text: And mock the deep-mouthed thunder. For at hand,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Not trusting to this halting legate here,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Whom he hath used rather for sport than need,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Is warlike John, and in his forehead sits

speaker: BASTARD
text: A bare-ribbed Death, whose office is this day

speaker: BASTARD
text: To feast upon whole thousands of the French.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Strike up our drums to find this danger out.

speaker: BASTARD
text: And thou shalt find it, dauphin, do not doubt.
